scenes.title: How a Compiler Works
scenes.subtitle: Turning human-readable code into machine instructions
scenes.label: Scene 1 / 9

scenes.title: Source Code
scenes.subtitle: A programmer writes high-level statements in languages like C++, Java, or Rust.
scenes.label: Scene 2 / 9

scenes.title: Compiler Pipeline Overview
scenes.subtitle: Information flows through a structured, multi-stage machine.
scenes.label: Scene 3 / 9

scenes.title: Lexical Analysis
scenes.subtitle: Convert raw characters into structured tokens.
scenes.label: Scene 4 / 9

scenes.title: Parsing
scenes.subtitle: Build an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) to define program structure.
scenes.label: Scene 5 / 9

scenes.title: Semantic Analysis
scenes.subtitle: Check node logic to ensure types match, functions exist, and constraints hold.
scenes.label: Scene 6 / 9

scenes.title: Optimization
scenes.subtitle: Produce faster and smaller code without changing program behavior.
scenes.label: Scene 7 / 9

scenes.title: Code Generation
scenes.subtitle: Translate the optimized structure into processor instructions and binary bytes.
scenes.label: Scene 8 / 9
